summary: German Renaissance painter and leader of the Danube School. Known for The Battle of Alexander at Issus (giant floating tablet, sun and crescent moon), Landscape with Footbridge (one of earliest pure oil landscapes), and St. George Overcoming the Dragon (dwarfed by forest).

works.name: The Battle of Alexander at Issus (1529)
works.description: Massive chaotic battle scene with floating tablet inscription, sun setting over Nile, crescent moon. Commissioned by Duke William IV of Bavaria. 'World landscape' style. Persians in red turbans resembling Turks. Wikipedia

images.caption: The Battle of Alexander at Issus

clues.clue: Massive battle scene with giant suspended/floating tablet inscription; sun setting over Nile, crescent moon in upper left; commissioned by William IV of Bavaria
examples: includes a giant suspended tablet explaining his painting The Battle of Alexander at Issus
the sun sets over the Nile on the right and a crescent moon is about to disappear in the top left

clues.clue: 'World landscape' style (panoramic); Persians dressed in red turbans to resemble Turks (anachronistic)
examples: anachronistically dresses a group of people in red turbans to resemble Turks
best-known example of this style of painting pioneered by Joachim Patinir

clues.clue: Depicts Alexander defeating Darius III at Battle of Issus
examples: depicted Alexander defeating Darius in The Battle of Issus

works.name: Landscape with Footbridge (1520)
works.description: Considered one of the first pure landscapes in Western oil painting. Shows a footbridge between a gatehouse and a larch tree.

clues.clue: One of the earliest pure landscapes in Western oil painting; shows a footbridge between gatehouse and larch tree
examples: his 1520 Landscape with Footbridge is considered to be one of the first pure landscapes in Western painting
shows the titular footbridge between a gatehouse and a larch tree

works.name: St. George Overcoming the Dragon
works.description: St. George slaying a dragon but dwarfed by a gigantic forest scene.

clues.clue: St. George slays dragon but occupies only small space on canvas dominated by gigantic forest
examples: this saint is dwarfed by a gigantic forest scene; however, he still plays his traditional role of slaying a dragon
The title action only takes up a small amount of space on a canvas that is dominated by a forest

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Leader of the Danube School. Pupil of Lucas Cranach the Elder. Engravings influenced the Little Masters. Painted St. Sebastian, Rest on the Flight into Egypt, St. Florian Abbey scenes, Nativity of the Virgin (set inside medieval cathedral), Annunciation. Celebrated Regensburg's expulsion of Jews in 1519.

clues.clue: Collaborated with Durer on monumental woodcut Triumphal Arch
examples: Durer, his pupils, and this artist created a monumental woodcut print known as the Triumphal Arch

clues.clue: Nativity of the Virgin set inside a medieval cathedral focusing on architectural details rather than the birth
examples: This artist unusually set his Nativity of the Virgin inside a medieval cathedral

comprehensive_summary:
Albrecht Altdorfer was a German Renaissance painter and leader of the Danube School. His most famous work is The Battle of Alexander at Issus (1529), a massive battle scene commissioned by Duke William IV of Bavaria. It features a giant floating tablet inscription, the sun setting over the Nile, and a crescent moon, in the 'world landscape' style pioneered by Joachim Patinir. The Persians are anachronistically dressed in red turbans.

His Landscape with Footbridge (1520) is considered one of the earliest pure landscapes in Western oil painting. St. George Overcoming the Dragon shows the saint and dragon dwarfed by a gigantic forest.

Altdorfer was a pupil of Lucas Cranach the Elder. He painted scenes for St. Florian Abbey, his Rest on the Flight into Egypt, and an unusual Nativity of the Virgin set inside a medieval cathedral. His engravings influenced the Little Masters. He collaborated with Durer on the monumental woodcut Triumphal Arch and celebrated Regensburg's expulsion of the Jews in 1519.
